Title: Create Wishlist/List
Preconditions: User logged in, no lists created
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to Lists or Wishlist page
2. [ACT] Click 'Create New List' button
3. [ACT] Enter list name: 'Summer Essentials'
4. [ACT] Optionally set privacy: Private or Shared
5. [ACT] Click 'Create'
6. [ASSERT] Verify list created
7. [ASSERT] Verify list appears in My Lists
Assertions:
[DOM] 'Create New List' button is visible
[DOM] Form displays: Name (required), Description (optional), Privacy (Private/Shared)
[DOM] Success message shown: 'List created' or equivalent
[DOM] List appears in My Lists section
[DOM] Can create multiple lists
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] mutation errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Create button missing; form missing required fields; list not appearing in My Lists after creation; success message not shown

Title: Add Product to List from Product Page
Preconditions: User logged in, list exists
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to product detail page
2. [ACT] Locate and click 'Add to List' or heart icon
3. [ASSERT] Verify list selector modal or dropdown opens
4. [ACT] Select list: 'Summer Essentials'
5. [ACT] Click 'Add'
6. [ASSERT] Verify success notification
Assertions:
[DOM] 'Add to List' button or heart icon visible on PDP
[DOM] List selector shows all user lists
[DOM] Option to create new list inline visible
[DOM] Success message: 'Added to Summer Essentials'
[STATE] Heart icon fills or changes color after add
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] mutation errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Add to List button missing; list selector does not open; success notification not shown; heart icon not updating state

Title: Add Product to List from Category Page
Preconditions: User on category page, list exists
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to category page
2. [ACT] Hover over product card
3. [ACT] Click heart or 'Add to List' icon on card
4. [ACT] Select list from selector
5. [ASSERT] Verify product added without leaving category page
Assertions:
[DOM] Add to List icon visible on product card (on hover or always)
[DOM] List selector appears inline
[DOM] Product added successfully (notification shown)
[STATE] Icon indicates product already in list after add
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] mutation errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Icon not visible on hover; list selector does not appear; page navigates away unexpectedly; icon state not updated

Title: View List Contents
Preconditions: User logged in, list has products
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to My Lists
2. [ACT] Click on list 'Summer Essentials'
3. [ASSERT] Verify list page opens
4. [ASSERT] Verify products display with all details
Assertions:
[DOM] List page shows all products
[DOM] Each product shows: Image, Name, Price, In Stock status
[DOM] Each product has: View Product link, Add to Cart button, Remove from List button
[DOM] List metadata shown: Name, Item count, Created date
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: List page not loading; products missing image or price; action buttons (Add to Cart, Remove) missing

Title: Add to Cart from List
Preconditions: User viewing list with products
Steps:
1. [NAV] Open list page
2. [ACT] Click 'Add to Cart' on a product in the list
3. [ASSERT] Verify success notification
4. [ACT] Click 'Add All to Cart' button (if available)
5. [ASSERT] Verify all list items added to cart
Assertions:
[DOM] Each product has Add to Cart button
[DOM] 'Add All to Cart' button present
[DOM] Success notification per product or bulk add
[STATE] Cart count updates after add
[DOM] Products remain in list after adding to cart
[DOM] Out of stock items show warning or are skipped
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] mutation errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Add to Cart button missing on list items; products removed from list after add; Add All to Cart fails silently

Title: Remove Product from List
Preconditions: Product in list
Steps:
1. [NAV] Open list page
2. [ACT] Click Remove or X icon on a product
3. [ASSERT] Verify confirmation prompt (if applicable)
4. [ACT] Confirm removal
5. [ASSERT] Verify product removed from list
6. [ASSERT] Verify list count decrements
Assertions:
[DOM] Remove button or X icon visible per product
[DOM] Confirmation prompt shown: 'Remove from Summer Essentials?'
[DOM] Product no longer shown in list after removal
[DOM] Success message: 'Removed from list'
[DOM] List item count decrements by 1
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] mutation errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Remove button missing; product still visible after removal; no confirmation prompt; list count not updated

Title: Rename/Edit List
Preconditions: List exists
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to My Lists
2. [ACT] Click Edit or Settings on list 'Summer Essentials'
3. [ACT] Change name to 'Summer 2026'
4. [ACT] Change description or privacy (optional)
5. [ACT] Click Save
6. [ASSERT] Verify list updated with new name everywhere
Assertions:
[DOM] Edit option available (pencil icon or menu)
[DOM] Form fields editable: Name, Description, Privacy
[DOM] List name 'Summer 2026' updated in My Lists and list header
[DOM] Products in list unaffected by rename
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Edit option missing; name change not persisting; list name not updated in all locations

Title: Delete List
Preconditions: List exists with products
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to list page
2. [ACT] Click Delete or menu > Delete
3. [ASSERT] Verify confirmation prompt warns about item count
4. [ACT] Confirm deletion
5. [ASSERT] Verify list removed from My Lists
6. [ASSERT] Verify products still in catalog
Assertions:
[DOM] Delete option available
[DOM] Confirmation prompt shows item count: 'Delete Summer 2026? (X items will be removed)'
[DOM] List no longer appears in My Lists after deletion
[DOM] Success message: 'List deleted'
[DOM] Products remain available in catalog
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Delete option missing; no confirmation prompt; list remains in My Lists after deletion; catalog products incorrectly removed

Title: Share List (Public Link)
Preconditions: List exists, sharing enabled
Steps:
1. [NAV] Open list page
2. [ACT] Click Share button
3. [ASSERT] Verify share options display: Public Link, Email
4. [ACT] Click 'Get Public Link'
5. [ACT] Copy generated link
6. [NAV] Open link in incognito or different browser
7. [ASSERT] Verify list accessible without login
Assertions:
[DOM] Share button visible
[DOM] Share modal shows Public Link and Email options
[DOM] Public link generated as shareable URL
[DOM] Opening link shows list in read-only mode
[STATE] Share modal shows list as 'Shared'
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Share button missing; public link not generated; shared list not accessible without login; shared link editable by external users

Title: Share List via Email
Preconditions: List exists
Steps:
1. [NAV] Open list page
2. [ACT] Click Share
3. [ACT] Select Email option
4. [ACT] Enter recipient email: friend@example.com
5. [ACT] Add optional message
6. [ACT] Click Send
7. [ASSERT] Verify email received with correct details
Assertions:
[DOM] Email share option available in share modal
[DOM] Form shows: Recipient email field, optional message field
[EMAIL] Recipient receives invitation email
[EMAIL] Email contains: List name, sender name, item count, view link
[DOM] Clicking link in email opens the list
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Email share option missing; email not delivered; email missing list details or view link

Title: Mark List as Private/Shared
Preconditions: List exists
Steps:
1. [NAV] Open list settings via Edit
2. [ACT] Toggle privacy: Private → Shared
3. [ACT] Save
4. [ASSERT] Verify list marked as Shared
5. [ACT] Toggle back to Private
6. [ASSERT] Verify shared links are now disabled
Assertions:
[STATE] Privacy toggle in list settings
[DOM] Changing to Shared shows 'Shared' badge on list
[DOM] Changing back to Private disables existing share links
[STATE] Badge reflects current privacy status (Private/Shared)
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Privacy toggle missing; status badge not updating; old share links remain active after reverting to Private

Title: Product Already in List Indicator
Preconditions: Product already added to list
Steps:
1. [NAV] Add product to a list
2. [NAV] Navigate back to product PDP
3. [ASSERT] Verify heart icon is filled or indicator is shown
4. [ACT] Hover over heart icon
5. [ASSERT] Verify tooltip shows 'In Summer Essentials' or similar
6. [ACT] Click filled heart icon
7. [ASSERT] Verify options: Remove from list or Add to different list
Assertions:
[STATE] Heart icon filled or highlighted if product is in any list
[DOM] Tooltip shows which list(s) the product is in
[DOM] Clicking filled heart shows: Remove from list / Add to another list
[DOM] Duplicate add to same list is prevented
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Heart icon not filled for product already in list; tooltip missing; no options shown on clicking filled heart

Title: Move Product Between Lists
Preconditions: Product in list A, list B exists
Steps:
1. [NAV] Open list A
2. [ACT] Click menu on product
3. [ACT] Select 'Move to...'
4. [ACT] Select list B
5. [ASSERT] Verify product moved to list B
6. [ASSERT] Verify product no longer in list A
Assertions:
[DOM] Move option available in product context menu
[DOM] List selector shows all available lists
[DOM] Product removed from list A
[DOM] Product added to list B (no duplicate in A)
[DOM] Success message shown
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Move option missing; product duplicated in both lists after move; product disappears from both lists

Title: List - Out of Stock Product Handling
Preconditions: List contains out of stock product
Steps:
1. [NAV] Add product to list while in stock
2. [ADMIN] Mark product out of stock via Admin SPA
3. [NAV] Open list
4. [ASSERT] Verify out of stock badge on product
5. [ACT] Attempt to Add to Cart
6. [ASSERT] Verify button is disabled or shows error
Assertions:
[DOM] Out of stock products remain in list
[DOM] Badge shows 'Out of Stock' on affected product
[STATE] Add to Cart button disabled or shows out-of-stock message
[DOM] Option to remove product from list still available
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ADMIN] Product marked out of stock visible in Admin SPA
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Out of stock product disappears from list; Add to Cart not disabled for OOS product; no OOS badge shown

Title: List Analytics - GA4 add_to_wishlist Event
Preconditions: GA4 configured, list exists
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to product page
2. [ACT] Click Add to List and select list
3. [WAIT] Wait for confirmation
4. [ASSERT] Open DevTools > Network tab
5. [ASSERT] Verify GA4 event 'add_to_wishlist' fired
6. [ASSERT] Inspect event parameters
Assertions:
[NETWORK] GA4 add_to_wishlist event fired on add action
[NETWORK] Event parameters: currency (USD), value (product price), items array (item_id, item_name, price)
[DOM] Event visible in GA DebugView
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GA4 event request present in network log
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: add_to_wishlist event not firing; event parameters missing currency, value, or items array

Title: Create Shared List
Preconditions: Logged in user who is a member of a B2B organization, on My Lists page
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to My Lists (/account/lists)
2. [ACT] Click 'Create New List'
3. [ACT] Enter list name: 'Team Supplies'
4. [ACT] Set visibility to 'Shared' (visible to org members)
5. [ACT] Click 'Create'
6. [ASSERT] Verify list created successfully
7. [ASSERT] Verify list is marked as 'Shared'
Assertions:
[DOM] Create list form includes visibility option: Private vs Shared
[DOM] List created with 'Shared' badge or indicator
[DOM] List appears in My Lists with Shared status
[DOM] Other org members can see this list (validated in B2C-LIST-017)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] mutation errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Shared visibility option missing; list created without Shared badge; Shared list not visible to other org members

Title: Org Member Sees Shared List
Preconditions: Shared list 'Team Supplies' created by User A; User B is a member of the same organization
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as User B (different org member from User A)
2. [NAV] Navigate to My Lists (/account/lists)
3. [ASSERT] Look for list 'Team Supplies' created by User A
Assertions:
[DOM] 'Team Supplies' list visible to User B in lists section
[DOM] Shared lists from other org members displayed (e.g., 'Shared with me' or 'Team Lists' section)
[DOM] List shows creator attribution (User A's name)
[DOM] User B can view contents of the shared list
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Shared list not visible to org member; list not showing creator name; org member cannot open shared list contents

Title: Add to Shared List
Preconditions: Shared list 'Team Supplies' visible to User B (org member)
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as User B
2. [NAV] Navigate to the shared list 'Team Supplies'
3. [ACT] Click 'Add Product' or navigate to a product and add it to 'Team Supplies'
4. [ASSERT] Verify product is added to the shared list
5. [NAV] Log in as User A (list creator)
6. [NAV] Navigate to 'Team Supplies'
7. [ASSERT] Verify the product added by User B is visible
Assertions:
[DOM] User B can add products to a shared list owned by User A
[DOM] Product added by User B appears in the shared list immediately
[DOM] When User A views the list, item added by User B is present
[DOM] Added item shows who added it (if attribution is supported)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] mutation errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: User B cannot add to shared list owned by another user; product added by User B not visible to User A; attribution missing

Title: Private List Not Visible to Org Members
Preconditions: User A has created a Private list; User B is a member of the same org
Steps:
1. [ASSERT] Verify User A has a list set to 'Private'
2. [NAV] Log in as User B (different org member)
3. [NAV] Navigate to My Lists
4. [ASSERT] Verify User A's private list is NOT visible to User B
Assertions:
[DOM] User A's Private list does not appear in User B's My Lists view
[DOM] No mechanism allows User B to discover or access User A's private list
[DOM] User B only sees their own lists and explicitly shared org lists
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Private list visible to other org members; private list accessible via guessed URL

Title: Saved for Later Page - View and Move Items Back to Cart
Preconditions: User logged in; at least one product has been saved for later from the cart (appears in /account/saved-for-later)
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/account/saved-for-later
2. [ASSERT] Verify page loads with heading 'Saved for Later' or equivalent
3. [ASSERT] Verify saved product is listed with: product image, name, price, and 'Move to Cart' action
4. [ACT] Click 'Move to Cart' on a saved item
5. [ASSERT] Verify item removed from Saved for Later list
6. [ASSERT] Verify success toast or inline confirmation: e.g., 'Item moved to cart'
7. [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
8. [ASSERT] Verify item now appears in cart
Assertions:
[DOM] /account/saved-for-later page loads without errors
[DOM] Saved item shows product image, name, and price
[DOM] 'Move to Cart' button or action visible per item
[STATE] After Move to Cart: item removed from Saved for Later list immediately
[DOM] Toast or confirmation: 'Item moved to cart' (or equivalent)
[DOM] Moved item appears in /cart with correct product details
[STATE] Cart item count in header increments by 1 after move
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] mutation errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
[NETWORK] no 4xx on saved-for-later page load or move-to-cart action
Failure_Signals: Page returns 404 or 500; saved items not listed; Move to Cart button missing; item not removed from saved-for-later after action; item not appearing in cart after move

Title: Saved for Later Page - Remove Item and Empty State
Preconditions: User logged in; at least one item in Saved for Later; empty state to be triggered
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/account/saved-for-later
2. [ASSERT] Verify at least one saved item is displayed
3. [ACT] Click the remove or delete action on the saved item
4. [ACT] Confirm removal if a confirmation dialog appears
5. [ASSERT] Verify item removed from list
6. [ASSERT] If list is now empty: verify empty state message is shown (e.g., 'No items saved for later' or similar call to action)
Assertions:
[DOM] Remove action (trash icon or 'Remove' link) visible per saved item
[STATE] After removal: item no longer shown in list
[DOM] List updates without full page reload
[DOM] When all items removed: empty state shown with descriptive message (not a blank page)
[DOM] Empty state may include a CTA such as 'Continue Shopping' or 'Browse Catalog'
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
[NETWORK] no 4xx on remove action
Failure_Signals: Remove action missing; item persisting after removal; blank page shown instead of empty state message; page reload required after removal

Title: List View - Pagination or Load More for Large Lists
Preconditions: User logged in; a list exists containing more than 10 products (trigger pagination or load-more)
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/account/lists
2. [ACT] Open a list that contains 11 or more products
3. [ASSERT] Observe how the product grid is presented - look for a 'Load More' button, infinite scroll trigger, or page navigation controls
4. [SCROLL] Scroll to the bottom of the current product grid
5. [ACT] If 'Load More' is visible: click it. If infinite scroll: continue scrolling.
6. [ASSERT] Verify additional products load (11th item and beyond become visible)
7. [ASSERT] Verify no duplicate products appear in the list
Assertions:
[DOM] Products beyond the initial page/batch visible after Load More or scroll
[DOM] No duplicate product entries after loading subsequent batches
[STATE] Total displayed product count eventually matches list item count
[DOM] If paginated: page indicator or 'Page X of Y' shown
[DOM] If Load More: button text changes or disappears after all items loaded (e.g., 'No more items')
[FORMAT] Product count badge on list matches total items across all pages
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
[NETWORK] pagination/load-more request returns 200 with non-empty data
[API] errors[] empty on list products query
Failure_Signals: List truncated at 10 items with no way to see more; duplicate products after loading more; Load More button not appearing when more items exist; item count badge not matching visible items

Title: Shared List - Remove Shared Access
Preconditions: Shared list with User B having access; User A is list owner
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as User A (list owner)
2. [NAV] Open the shared list settings
3. [ACT] Change list visibility from Shared to Private
4. [ACT] Save changes
5. [ACT] Click user name in top header → click Logout in popup (BL-AUTH-007; data-testid=main-layout.top-header.account-menu.sign-out-button")

Title: Shared List - Shared Indicator Displayed
Preconditions: User has both shared and private lists
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in and navigate to /account/lists
2. [ASSERT] Verify shared lists have a shared indicator (icon or badge or label)
3. [ASSERT] Verify private lists do NOT have the shared indicator
4. [ASSERT] Verify indicator styling is distinct and readable

[DOM] Shared lists display shared icon or Shared label
[DOM] Private lists have no shared indicator or show Private label
[FORMAT] Indicators are visually distinct between shared and private
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors

Title: Add Entire List to Cart - All Items Added
Preconditions: User logged in; list exists with 3+ products all in stock
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to /account/lists
2. [ACT] Open a list with 3+ products
3. [ACT] Click Add All to Cart button

5. [ASSERT] Verify all products from list added to cart

Assertions:

Title: Add List to Cart - Out of Stock Item Partial Add

Title: Add List to Cart - Preserves Quantities
Preconditions: User logged in; list with products that have set quantities
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to /account/lists
2. [ACT] Open a list with products
3. [ACT] Note quantity for each item (default is 1 unless list supports qty)
4. [ACT] Click Add All to Cart
5. [NAV] Navigate to cart
6. [ASSERT] Verify quantities in cart match list quantities
Assertions:
[MATH] Cart quantities match list quantities for each item
[DOM] Each cart line item quantity correct
[STATE] No quantity duplication or loss during transfer
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Quantities changed during add-to-cart; all items added with qty 1 regardless of list qty; quantity data lost
